observations.recommendation:
We recommend that officials concerned particularly the Acting General Services
Officer and its local Bids and Award Committee personnel to regularly and to adhere to
proper internal processes by providing the resident auditor in advance with copies of the
Purchase Orders and within 24 hours after acceptance of such procurement inform the
assigned audit team leader of the deliveries made.

The management shall prioritize registration with PhilGEPS not only to take
advantage of its built-in efficiencies but also avail of volume discounts inherent in
bulk purchasing. It may also promote transparency and efficiency in the
procurement process and give all interested parties an equal opportunity to bid.

We recommended that management observe the general guidelines stipulated in
COA Cir. No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013 RE: Information and Publicity of
Programs, Projects and Activities of government agencies. This is to promote Good
Governance through transparency and accountability.

We recommended that Municipal Engineer must request earlier for inspection of
various Infrastructure projects and enhance cooperation and support to the COA
Inspectorate Team from the Regional Technical and Information Technology Services in
the inspection of said project by providing it with the complete project requirements and
guiding the COA engineers to the project sites. Likewise the MPDC should strictly
follow the guidelines stated in the DILG and DBM Circular in programming the 20%
Local Development Fund.

a. Instructions shall also be given to the head of the accounting section to
maximize work assignment among accounting personnel, so as to
prevent work overload and meet deadlines on reports submission; and
b. Moreover, demand in writing, from the agency’s Collecting Officer and
Disbursing Officer to immediately prepare monthly reports of their
transactions and to submit the same together with its supporting
documents, to the Accounting Section within the time prescribed by the
regulations.

We recommended that management should require the municipal accountant and
municipal treasurer to observe strictly the schedule of rendition of accounts, in
accordance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-006, so that the Auditor could
perform timely audit of the financial transactions of the agency.

We recommended to the management for the immediate physical inventory taking
and reporting and require the accountant to reconcile the balances appearing in the report
with that of the concerned officer and effect the necessary adjustment after establishing
the correctness of the balance of the PPE account.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and MDRRMO to submit
regularly and on time all required reports needed in order for us to validate and verify the
transactions.

We recommended that the focal person should submit regularly and on time all
required reports needed in order for us to validate and verify the transactions.

We recommended that the LCE should:
a. ensure that there is an Approved Budget for SEF;
b. direct the Municipal Treasurer to maintain a separate depository
account for the SEF and regularly remit the SEF municipal share
thereof:
c. direct the Budget Officer to maintain a Registry for the SEF

observations.managementAction: complied the recommendation

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the concerned officials of the Municipality of Patikul to support all claims with sufficient and required documentations, needed particularly, on the utilization of the COVID 19 appropriated funds in their procurement of medical related goods and services. Likewise, management must strictly observe laws, rules and regulations as provided for in Section 4(6) of PD 1445, COA Cir. No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 on proper and sufficient required documentations and Section 52.1 of RA 9184 on Shopping as an alternative mode of procurement.
observations.managementAction: complied the recommendation

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the management direct the concerned accountant to observe the perpetual system in recording purchases and to use the RSMI in recording issuances and consumption of stocks and prepare adjusting entries for all errors in recording to correct the balances of inventory accounts.
observations.managementAction: The management complied the recommendation

observations.recommendation: We recommend that proper internal control processes for over supplies or property be adopted and it demands that, proper and accurate recording as to its valuation, existence, ownership and completeness shall be kept for all incoming and out-going supplies or property in pursuant to Sec. 42, 45 to 46 of NGAS Manual Vol. I
observations.managementAction: The management adopted and it demands that, proper and accurate recording as to its valuation, existence, ownership and completeness shall be kept for all incoming and out-going supplies or property

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management observe the general guidelines stipulated in COA Cir. This is to promote Good Governance through transparency and accountability.

We recommend Waste Management be formalize and change management strategy in the implementation of a realistic and doable Solid Waste Management Plan. Develop an Action/Implementation Plan. Draw an arrangement for the Plan and identify possible risks in a comprehensive manner in order to facilitate proper implementation, monitoring and measurement of results, and to establish accountability of the responsible person.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and MDRRMO to submit regularly and on time all required reports needed in order for us to validate and verify the transactions.
The preparation of the annual GAD Plan and the annual GAD Accomplishment Report shall be prepared by the GAD Focal Person and is subject to the guidelines issued by the agencies concerned.
Comply with Section 5 of the Property Insurance Law or the Property Insurance Law and other related issuances.

Recommendation:
We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the concerned officials of
the Municipality of Patikul to support all claims with sufficient and required
documentations, particularly, on the utilization of the COVID 19 appropriated funds in
their procurement of medical related goods and services. Likewise, management must
strictly observe laws, rules and regulations as provided for in Section 4(6) of PD 1445,
COA Cir. No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 on proper and sufficient required
documentations and Section 52.1 of RA 9184 on Shopping as an alternative mode of
procurement.

Recommendation
We recommend that the management direct the concerned accountant to observe
the perpetual system in recording purchases and to use the RSMI in recording issuances
and consumption of stocks and prepare adjusting entries for all noted errors in recording to
correct the balances of inventory accounts.

Recommendation:
We recommend that proper internal control over supplies or property be adopted
and it demands that, proper and accurate recording as to its valuation, existence,
ownership and completeness shall be kept for all incoming and out-going supplies or
property in pursuant to Sec. 42, 45 to 46 of NGAS Manual Vol. I

Likewise the MPDC should strictly follow the guidelines stated in the DILG and DBM Circular in programming the 20% Local Development Fund.

We recommend that management should require the municipal accountant and municipal treasurer to observe strictly the schedule of rendition of accounts, in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-006, so that the Auditor could perform timely audit of the financial transactions of the agency.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive directs the person(s) involved to strictly adhere to the timely compliance of the documents preceding the transaction as enumerated in the preceding paragraph pursuant to COA Circular 2009-001 and RA 9184. Moreover, as a control of the LGU, we enjoin the LCE to desist from approving the payment of contractual obligations without first ensuring that the contract documents have been submitted and received by the Audit Team.

The preparation of the annual GAD Plan and the annual GAD Accomplishment Report shall be prepared by the GAD Focal Person and is subject to the guidelines issued by the agencies concerned.

We recommended compliance with Section 5 of Republic Act 656 or the Property Insurance Law which states that a municipal government below first class may upon application insure its properties in the Fund under such rules and regulations as the System may prescribe.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal General Services Officer to submit the report of the physical inventory report of the PPE every year as mandated by law, and to maintain and update the property cards and reconcile the records with the Accountant; and to create an inventory committee that will focus on the conduct of the physical inventory of all properties of the LGU.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2017- Part II-No. noted the herein audit recommendation

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management require the Municipal General Services
Officer to submit the report of the physical inventory report of the PPE every
year as mandated by law, and to maintain and update the property cards and
reconcile the records with the Accountant; and to create an inventory
committee that will focus on the conduct of the physical inventory of all
properties of the LGU.

The audit team leader suggested that the LGU should have an inventory
committee per Office to facilitate the inventory taking and the assigned General
Service Officer to consolidate the report and conduct of ocular inspection to
validate the inventory. For Public Infrastructures the Local Chief Executive
mentioned that they will start and submit the inventory report.

We recommended that the:

a) Concerned officials should fast tract the implementation of PPAs covered by
these fund transfers in order to accomplish the purpose for which the funds
were granted and deliver immediate and optimum result to target beneficiaries;
b) Require the Municipal Accountant to facilitate the liquidation reports to the
source agency.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive directs the person(s) involved
to strictly adhere to the timely compliance of submission of the documents
enumerated in the preceding paragraph pursuant to COA Circular 2009-001 and RA
9184. Moreover, as a control of the LGU, we enjoin the LCE to desist from
approving the payment of contractual obligations without first ensuring that the
contract documents have been submitted and received by the Audit Team.

We recommended that management observe the general guidelines
stipulated in COA Cir. No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013 RE: Information
and Publicity of Programs, Projects and Activities of government agencies.
This is to promote Good Governance through transparency and accountability.

We recommend that management strictly comply with the relevant
provisions of COA Circular No. 2013-004 that are meant to uphold good local
governance through transparency and accountability, at least cost or effective
means; and require the Municipal Engineer to inform our office within ten (10)
days after the award of the Infrastructure projects on or before the start of the
projects/ programs/ activities (PPAs) that the appropriate projects signboards
and /or public notices are already posted at the project site, for proper
validation by this Office.

Recommendation:
The management shall see to it that all developmental projects were duly
verified/inspected by the authorized COA officials.

Recommendation
We recommend the management should strictly adhere on the
implementation of the Annual Investment Plan for the Calendar Year 2018
pursuant to Section 305(g) of the Republic Act 7160, otherwise known as
the Local Government code of the Philippines.

Recommendation
We recommend that management should adhere strictly to COA Circular
97-002 and Section 93 of PD 1445 requiring payments for government
transactions be made by check and payable directly to the creditor or thru cash
advance granted to the disbursing officer for specific purpose. Stop the practice
of disbursing government funds thru reimbursement to officials and employees.
Only Disbursing Officers are authorized to pay in cash through the cash
advance issued to them.

Management Comment
The management assured the audit team that they stop the practice of
disbursing government funds through reimbursement basis and will fully
comply with the above-cited provisions.

Recommendation
a. Instructions shall also be given to the head of the accounting section
to maximize work assignment among accounting personnel, so as to
prevent work overload and meet deadlines on reports submission;
and
b. Moreover, demand in writing, from the agency’s Collecting Officer
and Disbursing Officer to immediately prepare monthly reports of
their transactions and to submit the same together with its
supporting documents, to the Accounting Section within the time
prescribed by the regulations.

Recommendation
We recommend that the Solid Waste Management be formalize and change
management strategy in the implementation of a realistic and doable Solid
Waste Management Plan. Develop an Action/Implementation Plan. Draw an
arrangement for the Plan and identify possible risks in a comprehensive
manner in order to facilitate proper implementation, monitoring and
measurement of results, and to establish accountability of the responsible
person.

Recommendation
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and MDRRMO to submit
regularly and on time all required reports needed in order for us to validate and
verify the transactions.

We recommended that the: Concerned officials should fast tract the implementation of PPAs covered by these fund transfers in order to accomplish the purpose for which the funds were granted and deliver immediate and optimum result to target beneficiaries; and require the Municipal Accountant to facilitate the liquidation reports to the source agency.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management require the Municipal General Services Officer to submit the report of the physical inventory report of the PPE every year as mandated by law, and to maintain and update the property cards and reconcile the records with the Accountant; and to create an inventory committee that will focus on the conduct of the physical inventory of all properties of the LGU.
The audit team leader suggested that the LGU should have an inventory committee per Office to facilitate the inventory taking and the assigned General Service Officer to consolidate the report and conduct of ocular inspection to validate the inventory.  For Public Infrastructures the Local Chief Executive mentioned that they will start and submit the inventory report.

We recommended that the:
Concerned officials should  fast tract the implementation of PPAs covered by these fund transfers in order to accomplish the purpose for which the funds were granted and deliver immediate and optimum result to target beneficiaries; and
Require the Municipal Accountant to facilitate the liquidation reports to the source agency.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive directs the person(s) involved to strictly adhere to the timely compliance of submission of the documents enumerated in the preceding paragraph pursuant to COACircular 2009-001 andRA 9184. Moreover, as a control of the LGU, we enjoin the LCE to desist from approving the payment of contractual obligations without first ensuring that the contract documents have been submitted and received by the Audit Team.

We  recommend that management strictly comply with the relevant provisions of  COA Circular No. 2013-004 that are meant to uphold good local governance through transparency and accountability, at least cost or effective means; and require the Municipal Engineer to inform our office within ten (10) days after the award of the Infrastructure projects on or before the start of the projects/ programs/ activities (PPAs) that the appropriate projects signboards and /or public  notices are already posted at the project site, for proper validation by this Office.
The management shall see to it that all developmental projects were duly verified/inspected by the authorized COA officials.

We recommend that management should adhere strictly to COA Circular 97-002 and Section 93 of PD 1445 requiring payments for government transactions be made by check and payable directly to the creditor or thru cash advance granted to the disbursing officer for specific purpose.  Stop the practice of disbursing government funds thru reimbursement to officials and employees.  Only Disbursing Officers are authorized to pay in cash through the cash advance issued to them.
Instructions shall also be given to the head of the accounting section to maximize work assignment among accounting personnel, so as to prevent work overload and meet deadlines on reports submission; and
Moreover, demand in writing, from the agency’s Collecting Officer and Disbursing Officer to immediately prepare monthly reports of their transactions and to submit the same together with its supporting documents, to the Accounting Section within the time prescribed by the regulations.

We recommend that the Solid Waste Management be formalize and change management strategy in the implementation of a realistic and doable Solid Waste Management Plan.  Develop an Action/Implementation Plan.  Draw an arrangement for the Plan and identify possible risks in a comprehensive manner in order to facilitate proper implementation, monitoring and measurement of results, and to establish accountability of the responsible person.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and MDRRMO to submit regularly and on time all required reports needed in order for us to validate and verify the transactions.

We recommended that the LCE should:
ensure that there is an Approved Budget for SEF;
direct the Municipal Treasurer to maintain a separate depository account for the SEF and regularly remit the SEF municipal share thereof:
direct the Budget Officer to maintain a Registry for the SEF

observations.managementAction: Management noted the herein audit recommendation

observations.recommendation:

The management should instruct concerned officials to submit disbursement vouchers and other required reports promptly, to meet the deadline set by the Commission on Audit which is every 10th day of the ensuing month. Otherwise, we will be compelled to request for the withholding of salaries and other emoluments of concerned personnel until such time that the requirements of the Commission on Audit are being complied with (section 122(2),P.D. 1445);
Instructions shall also be given to the head of the accounting section to maximize work assignment among accounting personnel, so as to prevent work overload and meet deadlines on reports submission; and

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the transfer of the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF to aspecial trustfund which will be utilized solely for the purpose of supporting disaster risk reduction and management activities of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council (LDRRMC) within the next five (5) years.Strict compliance with law, rules and regulation on LDRRMF implementation shall be strictly observed.

We further recommended that, the management must prepare Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP) in support of disaster risk reduction and management activities of the LDRRMC, as stated in COA Circular No. 2012-002. Management must also evaluate the basic needs as against the capability to timely procure and distribute goods or deliver on time, needed social/health services during crisis time.  This effective and efficient delivery of immediate concerns during crisis time can best be addressed following the guidelines and procedures in the adoption of an appropriate disaster preparedness plan incorporating all crisis preparedness and prevention activities subject to the declaration of imminent danger of calamity by the local sanggunian.

Further, transfer to special trust fund account the equivalent amount and follow regulation in its utilization.
observations.managementAction: Management noted the herein audit recommendation

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the Municipal General Services Officer to submit the report of the physical inventory report of the PPE every year as mandated by law, and to maintain and update the property cards and reconcile the records with the Accountant; and to create an inventory committee that will focus on the conduct of the physical inventory of all properties of the LGU.

We hereby recommended the following:

That the Bids and Awards Committee shall comply with the regulations on procurement through shopping. Procurement above the threshold should be done thru procurement service or public bidding pursuant to the provisions under RA 9184 to foster transparency and competitiveness and ensure the prices are the most advantageous to the municipality. However, splitting by dividing the projects into artificial phases to circumvent the regulations should not be resorted to.

Stop the payment of purchases out of reimbursement basis unless circumstances warrant its application.

Required documents on procurement shall be complete, properly numbered, signed and dated.

Include in the Annual Procurement Plan (APP), purchases of construction materials and other construction paraphernalia and make revision on every changes made with APP to suit the mode of procurement adopted.

We recommended that the:
Concerned officials should  fast tract the implementation of PPAs covered by these fund transfers in order to accomplish the purpose for which the funds were granted and deliver immediate and optimum result to target beneficiaries; and
Require the Municipal Accountant to facilitate the liquidation reports to the source agency.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive directs the person(s) involved to strictly adhere to the timely compliance of submission of the documents enumerated in the preceding paragraph pursuant to COA Circular 2009-001 and RA 9184. Moreover, as a control of the LGU, we enjoin the LCE to desist from approving the payment of contractual obligations without first ensuring that the contract documents have been submitted and received by the Audit Team.

We recommended that management observe the general guidelines stipulated in COA Cir. No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013 RE: Information and Publicity of Programs, Projects and Activities of government agencies.  This is to promote Good Governance through transparency and accountability.
The management shall see to it that all developmental projects were duly verified/inspected by the authorized COA officials.

We recommended that:
The management should instruct concerned officials to submit disbursement vouchers and other required reports promptly, to meet the deadline set by the Commission on Audit which is every 10th day of the ensuing month. Otherwise, we will be compelled to request for the withholding of salaries and other emoluments of concerned personnel until such time that the requirements of the Commission on Audit are being complied with (section 122(2),P.D. 1445);
Instructions shall also be given to the head of the accounting section to maximize work assignment among accounting personnel, so as to prevent work overload and meet deadlines on reports submission; and
Moreover, demand in writing, from the agency’s Collecting Officer and Disbursing Officer to immediately prepare monthly reports of their transactions and to submit the same together with its supporting documents, to the Accounting Section within the time prescribed by the regulations.

Develop an Action/Implementation Plan.  Draw an arrangement for the Plan and identify possible risks in a comprehensive manner in order to facilitate proper implementation, monitoring and measurement of results, and to establish accountability of the responsible person.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and MDRRMO to submit regularly and on time all required reports needed in order for us to validate and  verify the transactions.

direct the Budget Officer to maintain a Registry for the SEF

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:
Include in the yearly action plan the conduct of physical count on all PPE, as this activity in very important in checking actual existence of the assets and determining its usefulness and current condition as well as pinpoint custodial responsibilities; 
Create an Inventory Committee to take charge in the overall physical count on PPE and submit corresponding reports to the office of the auditor immediately thereafter; and
Maintain/provide General Ledgers, Subsidiary Ledgers (Equipment Ledger Cards) on PPE accounts and reconcile balances with property records and likewise with Report on Physical Count of PPE to be able to reflect complete and accurate PPE balance in the financial statements.
